subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation
Research question is clearly stated, well-focused and effectively connected to all sections
Appropriate range of relevant sources and methods with clear evidence of effective and informed selection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the topâband descriptors. The report immediately frames the issue in context and consistently returns to a narrowly scoped, well-formulated research question. The methodology is varied and justified with reference to both business-management theory and primary/secondary data needs, demonstrating clear, informed selection and implementation of methods.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent subject knowledge of CSR theory and financial analysis
Terminology is used accurately and consistently throughout
Source material is for the most part clearly relevant and appropriately applied to support arguments
weaknesses: Several quantitative claims (e.g. tourism loss figure, spill volume) lack full citation or methodological clarification
Omission of industry benchmarking limits the rigor of some financial comparisons
Some bibliography entries lack retrieval dates or complete publication details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Subject knowledge and use of terminology consistently meet the top band, and most sources are integrated effectively. However, lapses in citation rigor, missing methodological detail, and omitted benchmarks prevent a full-band score, placing the work solidly in the 5/6 range.

strengths: Good range of primary and secondary data clearly linked to the research question
Analysis is logically structured with supported conclusions drawn from quantitative and qualitative evidence
Argument is coherent and acknowledges limitations, with partial critical evaluation
weaknesses: Occasional confirmatory bias in the framing of objectives
Lack of industry comparators reduces comparative depth
Alternative causal factors (e.g. oil-price fluctuations) are under-developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates clear relevance of research methods to the question and offers well-supported analysis, but sophistication is constrained by missing benchmarks and somewhat narrow evaluation of alternative explanations. This places it at the lower end of the 7â9 band.

strengths: Structure fully meets IB expectations with clear headings, numbered sections, and logical flow
Layout, formatting, pagination, and referencing are consistently applied to a professional standard
weaknesses: Mathematical expressions are inconsistently formatted (MathML vs LaTeX)
One table header is duplicated, which may confuse readers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required presentation strands are met to a high standard. Minor formatting inconsistencies (especially in the display of formulas and a duplicated column header) do not significantly impair readability, justifying full marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is stated verbatim on the cover page and again in the introduction, is sufficiently narrow (one company, one incident, one financial indicator) and is revisited in all analytical sections and in the conclusion.  â¢  Subsidiary objectives are logically derived (e.g., STEEPLE, stakeholder matrix, ratio analysis) and each analytical tool is explicitly tied back to answering the central question, illustrating a strong focus.  â¢  Although the hypothesis pre-supposes a negative impact, the wording of the RQ itself remains open to any direction of influence, fulfilling the descriptor for a well-focused, effectively connected research question.

The student constructs a reasoned argument that CSR spending negatively affected short-term profit, synthesising qualitative stakeholder findings with quantitative financial impacts.  â¢  Evaluation acknowledges limitations (communication gaps, possible price effects) and identifies unanswered questions for future research, showing partial critical reflection.  â¢  Because evaluation is present but not exhaustive and alternative explanations are under-developed, the work meets the âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluationâ standard, warranting the mid-upper end of the 7-9 band.

The student demonstrates clear, coherent understanding of CSR theories (Bowen, Freeman), stakeholder management, STEEPLE analysis and profitability ratios, accurately applying them to Repsolâs context.  â¢  Explanations of how each concept links to financial outcomes show solid command of Business-Management knowledge, and the student correctly interprets complex figures such as adjusted clean-up costs and their ratio impact.  â¢  Occasional over-statement of causality is acknowledged, but overall subject knowledge is strong and effectively supported by sources, justifying the top band.

Business-management terminology (e.g., âhorizontal analysisâ, âgross-profit marginâ, âstakeholder power-interest matrixâ) is used accurately and consistently in both Spanish and English sections.  â¢  Key theoretical constructs are defined with scholarly references before application, and formulas are correctly notated.  â¢  Minor formatting inconsistency in LaTeX does not undermine conceptual precision; therefore use of terminology fully meets the descriptor for accurate and consistent application.

Analytical techniques are correctly executedâratio calculations, trend graphs and stakeholder assessmentsâand conclusions are generally supported by quantitative evidence (e.g., 444 % rise in expenses explains net-profit fall).  â¢  Nevertheless, causal linkage is sometimes asserted rather than proven, and benchmark absence limits comparative insight, so the sophistication falls short of the 10-12 descriptor but rises above a merely âclearâ analysis.  A lower-mid score within the 7-9 band is therefore appropriate.

Most evidence cited (financial statements, OEFA beach reports, government press releases, academic theory texts) is directly relevant to the research question, and the student generally integrates quotations and data appropriately in support of arguments.  â¢  Nonetheless, a handful of key quantitative claims â notably the S/200 million tourism loss and the exact spill volume â are repeated without in-text citation or methodological clarification, and industry benchmarking of ratios is omitted.  These lapses prevent the source use from being consistently rigorous, aligning better with the âmostly relevant and appropriateâ 3-4 band.

A mixed-methods design is outlined, combining qualitative tools (STEEPLE, stakeholder power-interest, circular-business-model audit) with quantitative methods (horizontal analysis, ratio analysis, trend graphs).  â¢  The essay justifies each tool with reference to Business-Management theory and selects primary corporate financial statements directly from Repsol and regulatory bodies, along with a wide range of secondary sources (government releases, OEFA, UN, reputable media).  â¢  Source selection is clearly purposefulâfinancial statements are used for profitability metrics while governmental/NGO documents inform the environmental and social dimensionsâshowing clear evidence of effective and informed methodological choice.

The investigation draws on a good variety of primary financial data and authoritative secondary documents that are explicitly linked to each analytical section, demonstrating clear relevance to the RQ.  â¢  Depth is constrained by the absence of industry comparators and alternative causal factors (oil-price shocks), preventing the research from reaching the âexcellentâ standard, yet it is more than merely adequate.  A solid but not flawless alignment with the RQ positions the work comfortably in the 7-9 band.

Overall formatting is professionalâpage numbering, word count, consistent font and spacing, Harvard citations and clear figure/table labelling are provided.  â¢  Minor issues such as missing axis units and a duplicated table heading (âDatos del 2021â) are noted but do not significantly hinder readability; thus layout considerations are substantially met, corresponding to the upper band.

The studentâs topic â the influence of Repsolâs CSR response to the 2022 oil-spill on its period profit â is introduced immediately, framed in real-world context and repeatedly referenced across the essay.  â¢  The introduction explains why the issue is significant for multiple stakeholders and for Business Management as a discipline, and the table of contents and section overviews confirm that each chapter is designed to address the stated focus.  â¢  Definitions of key constructs (CSR, stakeholder theory, profit measures, STEEPLE, etc.) are supplied before they are used, demonstrating a deliberate effort to communicate the topic comprehensively.  Overall, communication is accurate, purposeful and complete, matching the 5-6 band.

The report follows IB EE structural conventions impeccably: title page, abstract elements, contents page, numbered headings, chapters, conclusion and references, facilitating logical flow.  â¢  All required components are present; internal pagination and heading hierarchy are clear, fulfilling the criteria for a fully appropriate structure.

annotations.comment: The adaptation of the circular economy diagram from Repsol is well-integrated, enhancing conceptual understanding of the comparison between linear and circular models.
The net margin equation is not formatted in LaTeX; enclose it in $$ for uniform presentation of all mathematical content.
The description of the STEEPLE analysis scope is concise and demonstrates clear understanding of its components and relevance to the case study.
Figure 4 clearly illustrates net-profit fluctuations with a mean reference line, strengthening the analysis of CSR spendingâs financial impact.
Section headings and numbering are clearly applied, creating a logical hierarchy that enhances clarity in presenting theoretical definitions.

The objective statement âbusca comprobar que la aplicaciÃ³n de la estrategia de RSC influyÃ³ de manera negativaâ introduces confirmatory bias; consider rephrasing to remain open to all possible outcomes.
The definition of âSostenibilidadâ lacks a complete publication date and page reference; ensure each source in the theoretical framework is fully cited for academic rigor.
Long, dense list items hinder readability; consider breaking these into shorter paragraphs or bullet points with subheadings for clarity.
Figure 3 effectively visualizes sales trends against the mean, supporting a clear quantitative comparison across the three years.
The student includes a precise word count at the start of the report, meeting the IB requirement for declaring word count clearly and accurately.
The research question is clearly stated on the title page and is narrowly focused on a single company, incident, and financial measure, providing excellent alignment with the studyâs aims.
The title of the investigation is prominently placed at the start of the report, fulfilling the formatting requirement for a clearly stated title.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, facilitating navigation and demonstrating effective presentation of the reportâs organization.
Page numbering is present and consistent in the footer, enhancing readability and professionalism throughout the document.
The introduction provides clear contextual background on Repsol and the 2022 oil spill, demonstrating strong subject knowledge and effectively setting up the research question.
Mathematical expressions are displayed via MathML tags rather than using LaTeX syntax; enclose all formulas within $$ ... $$ for consistency and readability.
The formula for gross profit margin is embedded as MathML instead of LaTeX; to maintain presentation consistency, use $$\frac{ganancia bruta}{ventas}\times100$$.
The reported spill volume of âmÃ¡s de 11 900 barrilesâ lacks an in-text citation for verification; include a precise reference to validate this key quantitative claim.
The stakeholder power-interest matrix visual is clear but lacks a textual legend defining each quadrantâs criteria; add explicit labels to reinforce reader comprehension.
The S/200 million tourism loss figure is presented without methodological explanation or full source details; specify its calculation basis and cite the source precisely.
The explanation of Repsolâs circular business model draws on the company website but lacks specific section or page citations; include precise source pointers.
Column header âDatos del 2021â is duplicated in Table 2, which may confuse readers; correct the label for the third column to reflect 2022 data accurately.
The expense breakdown in Figure 5 offers detailed insight into incident-related cost drivers, supporting a nuanced understanding of the financial impact.
The inclusion of gross and net profit margins succinctly quantifies the CSR investmentâs effect on profitability, demonstrating effective application of financial ratios.
The conclusion synthesizes qualitative and quantitative findings to answer the research question directly, reflecting a well-reasoned and critical evaluation.
The critique of the contingency plan misapplication is compelling but relies on a single source; incorporate additional evidence or viewpoints to deepen critical evaluation.
Table 1 omits data for 2020, creating a gap in trend analysis; consider including 2020 figures or explicitly explaining why they were excluded.
The references list is comprehensive and follows a consistent Harvard citation style, underpinning the investigationâs academic rigor.
Several online sources in the bibliography lack retrieval dates; include access dates for web references to fully meet citation standards.
